description: In an apartment living room, Rachel and Ross stand embracing. Behind them, Chandler stands wearing a shirt and tie, and Joey sits at a desk, resting his chin on his hand. A speaker whose mouth is not visible says, "Um."
Ross and Rachel continue their embrace. Ross rests his hands on Rachel's back.
Rachel's face rests against Ross's shoulder as she says, "oh,"
Rachel smiles while hugging Ross. Chandler and Joey maintain their positions in the background.
Ross and Rachel gently sway back and forth in their embrace.
Rachel leans back slightly, her hands on his shoulders, and says, "this is good. This is really good." Ross replies, "I know."
Ross holds Rachel's shoulders and says, "I know. It's it's almost"
Ross lowers his head, rubs his face with one hand, then looks back at Rachel.
Ross places his hands near Rachel's waist as they stand facing each other.
Ross gestures briefly toward Chandler and Joey, saying, "what do you say we go take a walk? Just us. Not them."
Rachel smiles and nods at Ross.
Rachel turns toward the door and says, "Okay. Let me get my coat."
Ross quickly steps in front of her, extending both hands forward, and says, "Okay. No. Hey, whoa. I'll get your coat."
Rachel stops, smiles, and says, "Okay?"
Ross turns and walks toward the apartment door. Rachel follows a few steps behind him.
Rachel closes the door after Ross exits. A faint voice says, "Whoops" as Rachel leans her back against the door.
Rachel turns her head back toward the room with a wide smile, pointing her thumb over her shoulder toward the hallway.
Rachel hops slightly in place, clenching her fists, and says, "going to get my coat? He's going to get my coat. Oh, my goodness."
Rachel walks quickly toward the desk, saying, "I can believe this is unbelievable."
Rachel stops beside the desk, looking directly at a laptop computer screen.
Rachel points at the laptop and asks, "What's that?" Chandler swiftly steps in front of the laptop, presses a key, and says, "What? Nothing."
Chandler places his hand on top of the closed laptop lid. Rachel leans forward, keeping her gaze on him.
Rachel points her finger at Chandler and says, "What is it? I saw my name. What is it?" Chandler shakes his head and says, "No, see?"
Chandler points toward a printer on the desk.
The mechanical sound of a printer operating begins. Joey stands up quickly, his eyes widening, and says, "Hey, it's printing."
Chandler pulls a piece of paper from the printer, turning away from it. He says, "Hey, it's printing," raising his volume compared to his previous line.
Chandler holds the paper against his side. Rachel gestures toward it as the apartment door opens and Ross walks in, carrying a jacket.
Rachel points to the paper and says, "What is it? Let me see." Ross approaches them, holding out the jacket, and says, "Hey, someone order a coat?"
Rachel gestures at Chandler and says to Ross, "Chandler wrote something about me on his computer and he won't let me see." Ross looks at Chandler and asks, "He won't?" Chandler replies, "He won't"
Ross raises a finger, pointing it directly at Chandler.
Ross gestures with his hand and asks Chandler, "because isn't that the short story you were writing?"
Chandler looks at Ross and nods his head once.
Chandler says, "Yes, it is the short story that I was writing."
Rachel reaches toward the paper, saying, "In a minute. Let me read it." Chandler steps back, pulling the paper away, and says, "No."
Ross steps closer to Chandler, gesturing toward Rachel.
Ross says to Chandler, "Come on. Hey, why don't you read it to her?"
Chandler glances at Ross, then lowers his gaze to the paper in his hand. Joey watches Chandler from the side.
Ross and Joey stand still, looking at Chandler. Chandler exhales audibly, his lips pressed together.
Chandler looks up and says, "All right."
Chandler grips the paper with both hands. Rachel walks over to an armchair and sits down, crossing her legs.
Chandler shifts his stance, raising the paper closer to his face. Ross and Joey stand nearby, watching him.
Chandler looks at the paper and says, "It was summer"
Chandler pauses, keeping his eyes on the paper.
Chandler reads, "and it was hot."
Rachel sits still in the chair, her gaze fixed on Chandler.
Chandler continues reading, "Rachel was there"
Rachel tilts her head slightly, maintaining her gaze on Chandler.
Chandler reads, "a lonely gray couch."
Chandler shifts his gaze further down the page.
Chandler reads, "Oh, look. Cried Ned"
Chandler pauses his reading.
Chandler continues, "and then the kingdom was his forever."
Chandler looks up from the paper and says, "The end."
Rachel turns her head toward Ross, her facial muscles relaxed. Ross stares forward without moving. Joey crosses his arms.
Rachel looks back at Chandler and asks, "That's it? That's all you wrote?"
Joey, with his arms still crossed, says, "You're the worst writer in the whole world."
Rachel quickly stands up from the chair.
Rachel points sharply toward Chandler and says, "All right, you know what? This isn't funny anymore."
Rachel steps toward Chandler, maintaining her pointing gesture, and says, "There's something about me on that piece of paper, and I want to see it."
Chandler pulls the paper back toward his chest and says, "No, you don't."
Rachel raises her hands briefly, then turns her back to Chandler, taking a step away.
Rachel faces the others and says, "You know what? Fine. If you guys want to be children about this, that's fine. I do not need"
Rachel suddenly lunges back toward Chandler, grabbing at the paper. Chandler, Ross, and Joey scramble over the furniture as Rachel snatches the paper from Chandler's hand.
Rachel backs away from the men, unfolding the paper. Ross, Chandler, and Joey stop moving, leaning against the back of the couch.
Rachel looks at the paper and asks, "what is this,"
Rachel lowers the paper slightly and looks directly at Ross.
Rachel says, "Ross?"
Rachel asks, "What is this?" Ross turns to Chandler, pats his shoulder, says "Thank you. Good luck," and begins to walk away.
Ross walks quickly toward the apartment door, opens it, and steps into the hallway.
Ross steps back into the doorway, raising both hands with his palms open, and says, "Okay, just remember how crazy I am about you, okay?"
Rachel looks down at the paper and reads aloud, "Kind of ditzy. I was too into her looks."
Ross raises a finger, pointing it toward Rachel.
Ross says, "Spoiled? Now, that's a little spoiled."
Ross points toward Chandler and says, "He was supposed to type little. The idiot."
Ross lowers his head, his gaze shifting downward, as the clip ends.
In the apartment kitchen, Rachel stands holding a piece of paper, her back to Ross.
Rachel says, "Just a waitress?"
Ross opens his mouth to speak, raising his right hand slightly.
Ross gestures with his right hand as he says, "Now, that that was I mean, as opposed to..."
Ross turns and steps away from Rachel, then turns back to face her.
Ross gestures with both hands and asks, "okay, is this over yet?"
Rachel turns to face him, gesturing downward toward her legs, and says, "Ross, I do not have chubby ankles." Audience laughter is audible.
Ross steps closer, waving his hands. "No, rach. Ridiculous. Okay, okay, look at the other side. Look at Julie's column."
Rachel looks at the paper and says, "She's not Rachem, what the hell's a Rachem?" Audience laughter follows.
Rachel glares at him and says, "That's a stupid, paleontology word that I wouldn't know because I'm just a waitress."
She walks past him toward the hallway. Ross follows, saying, "Rachel, come on."
Rachel walks out of the apartment, pulling the door shut behind her. Audience laughter is heard.
Ross opens the door, stepping into the hallway, and calls out, "Rach. Rachel?"
Ross leans against the closed door of apartment 20 and says, "No, she's not Rachel."
Ross turns his head and presses his face closer to the door.
Ross continues, "She's not..."
Ross stands still, remaining pressed against the door.
Ross finishes softly, "Rachel."

Ross closes his eyes and rests his head against the door. Instrumental transition music plays as the setting changes to a coffee shop, where Chandler, Monica, Phoebe, and Joey are seated around a coffee table. Rain falls outside the window.
Chandler gestures to his chest with both hands and says, "My diary. My diary."

Chandler continues speaking rapidly, "That's brilliant. I should have told her was my diary. She never would have made me read her my diary." Monica begins to respond, "No, that's..." Audience laughter is heard.
Monica finishes, "true. You know? You'd be a great person to have around the day after an emergency." Audience laughter follows.
Phoebe shakes her head slightly side-to-side.
Phoebe says, "I cannot believe Ross even made this list." Monica replies, "I know."
Monica adds, "What a dinkus."
Joey, holding an orange mug in an armchair, leans forward slightly.
Joey says, "Hey, cut him some slack."
Joey points his hand toward Chandler and says, "It was Chandler's idea." Monica and Phoebe turn to look at Chandler. Phoebe asks, "What?" Audience laughter is heard.
Chandler sits back, his eyes widening.
Chandler says, "Oh, good. I was hoping that would come up." Monica asks, "This was your idea?" Audience laughter continues.
Chandler gestures outward with his hands, "All right, let's get some..."
Chandler continues, "perspective here, okay? These things, they happen for a reason."
Monica points at him and says, "Yeah. You." Audience laughter follows.
Chandler turns his head toward Phoebe.
Chandler asks, "All right, phoebes, back me up here, okay? You believe in that karma crap, don't you?"
Phoebe looks at him and responds, "By the way, good luck in your next life as a..." Audience laughter builds.
Phoebe finishes, "...dung beetle."

Audience laughter peaks as Chandler sits silently. Instrumental transition music plays as a brief view of a rainy street at night appears. Inside her apartment, Rachel sits in an armchair and drops a tissue box onto her head.
Rachel lowers her arms and eats a small item from her hand, chewing slowly while staring forward.
Rain continues to fall against the window behind Rachel. Outside the glass, Ross appears on the fire escape, pulling his jacket tighter in the rain.
Rachel turns her head toward the window, notices Ross, and stands up from the armchair.
Ross stands outside in the rain, waving a piece of paper and gesturing toward the glass as audience laughter is heard.
Through the glass, Ross shouts, "Hey, open up. Please."
Rachel shouts back, "When? When somebody does not buzz you in Ross, that means, go away."
She continues, "That doesn't mean, Please climb up the..." Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel finishes, "...fire escape." Ross shouts, "Yeah, I just want to read you something. It's your pro list." Rachel replies, "Not interested," and walks away from the window.
Ross presses the piece of paper against the wet glass.
Ross shouts loudly through the window, "Okay. Okay. Number one, the way you cry at game shows."
Rachel paces around the living room. Ross continues shouting, "Number two how much you love your friends."
Ross adds, "Number three the way you play with your hair when you're..."

Monica, Chandler, Joey, and Phoebe enter the apartment through the front door, wearing raincoats. Ross continues, "...nervous. Number four how brave you are for starting a life over." Audience laughter follows their entrance.
The group walks further inside as Ross shouts, "Number five how great you are with..."
Ross finishes, "...Ben. Number six the way you smell."
Joey turns toward the window and asks loudly, "Hey, Ross, what are you doing?" Audience laughter is heard.
Ross asks, "Hey, Joe, you want to open the window?"
Joey says, "Oh, yeah," and begins walking toward the window.
Joey stops near the window. Meanwhile, Chandler opens the front door of the apartment.
Ross stumbles through the open front door, soaking wet, as audience laughter peaks. Chandler asks, "What are you doing out there?"
Monica wraps a blanket around Ross and says, "Oh, my gosh, you must be freezing. You know what you need? How about a nice steaming cup of hot chocolate?"
Ross stares forward silently, water dripping from his hair and face, while Monica holds the blanket around his shoulders.
In an apartment hallway, Ross, wearing a wet black jacket, stands at a closed purple door. He knocks on the door.
Ross continues knocking and says, "Rach, come on, open up."
Ross says, "Rach, come on. Come on. You got to give me another." In the living room behind him, Monica, Chandler, Joey, and Phoebe stand wearing raincoats, watching him.
The purple door opens, revealing Rachel.
Rachel shakes her head slightly and says, "No."
Ross says, "No."
Rachel replies, "That's what I said."
Chandler says, "Look, maybe we should go," as audience laughter is heard. Rachel gestures toward the group and says, "No, you guys, you really don't have to go. We're done talking."
Ross steps forward, saying, "Come on. Look, I know how you must" Rachel holds her hand up and interrupts, "no, you"
Rachel points at him and finishes, "don't, Ross." Audience laughter plays.
Rachel lowers her hand and places both hands on her upper chest.
Rachel says, "Imagine the worst things you think about yourself."
Rachel gestures with both hands and says, "Now, how would you feel if the one person that you trusted the most in the world..."
Rachel gestures broadly again, "...not only thinks them too but actually"
Rachel says, "uses them as reasons not to be with you?"
Ross stares at her, his mouth slightly parted.
Ross says, "I want to be with you in spite of all those things."
Rachel says, "Oh, well, that's mighty big of you, Ross." Audience laughter is heard. Rachel points and says, "I said, don't"
Rachel says, "go."
Chandler, Joey, Monica, and Phoebe walk past Ross toward the open door, their coats rustling.
Ross gestures with his hands, saying, "You know what? You know what? If things were the other way around..."
Ross continues, "...there is nothing you could put on a list that would that would ever"
Ross lowers his hands and says, "make me not want to be with you."
Rachel looks away, then meets his gaze. She says, "Well, then I guess that's the difference between us. See, I'd never make a list." Audience laughter plays.
Rachel closes the door. Ross is left standing in the hallway, holding a folded piece of paper.
Ross lowers his head, turns away from the door, and slowly walks into the living room.
Ross sits on the edge of the coffee table. Monica, Chandler, Joey, and Phoebe stand silently in a line behind the couch.
Joey looks at Chandler, then at Phoebe, before opening his mouth to speak.
Joey asks, "I never know how long you're supposed to wait in this type of situation before you can talk again." Audience laughter is heard.
Chandler turns his head toward Joey.
Chandler says, "Maybe a little longer." More laughter is heard.
Ross remains seated on the table, staring forward. The scene changes to a city street with yellow taxis driving in the rain; traffic sounds are audible.

In an office, Monica stands holding a plate of food. Mr. Rastatter sits at his desk. Monica says, "Now, in some of these recipes the quantities may seem just a little unusual. Like these coconut mocklet holiday nut"
Monica hands Mr. Rastatter a small card and continues, "bars. I've indicated four cups of coconut and four cups of crushed nut..."
Monica finishes, "...and only one tablespoon of mocklet."
Mr. Rastatter takes the card and looks at it.
Mr. Rastatter says, "Doesn't matter." Monica asks, "What?" Audience laughter plays.
Mr. Rastatter says, "Our FDA approval didn't come through?"
Mr. Rastatter leans back in his chair.
Mr. Rastatter says, "Something about laboratory rats." Audience laughter is heard again.
Monica looks at him with wide eyes.
Monica says, "Oh, gosh, I'm sorry."
Mr. Rastatter leans forward and picks up an envelope from the desk.
Mr. Rastatter extends the envelope to Monica, saying, "Yeah, well, anywho, here's your check."
Monica takes the envelope.
Mr. Rastatter stands up and says, "Thank you for all the trouble you went through."
Monica turns around and begins walking toward the door.
Mr. Rastatter points at her back and says, "Listen, you didn't eat a lot of it while you were cooking, did you?"
Monica stops, turns back, and says, "Well, I ate some." Mr. Rastatter says, "Oh, some."
Mr. Rastatter waves his hand and says, "That's fine. Some is fine. Some is not a lot."
Mr. Rastatter smiles slightly.
Mr. Rastatter asks, "Okay, so it doesn't burn when you pee, does it?" Audience laughter plays.
Monica stares at him silently, her lips slightly parted.
The scene changes to a dark city street with cars driving in the rain, their headlights reflecting on the wet road. Rain and traffic sounds are heard.
The scene changes to Monica's living room. Rachel sits on the window seat. Monica sits in an armchair holding a cordless phone and a pen. A telephone rings.
In his apartment, Ross sits on a couch holding a green bottle. He brings a cordless phone to his ear and says, "Hello?"
Monica speaks into her phone, "Hi, you him?"
Rachel turns her head toward Monica.
Rachel says, "Is that him again? Tell him I'd come to the phone, but my ankles are weighing me down." Audience laughter is heard.
Monica briefly closes her eyes and leans her head back, then brings the phone back to her ear.
Monica says, "Listen, I don't think this is the best time" Ross is shown on the phone, saying, "look, can you do something for me?" Monica is shown again and replies, "Sure."
Monica asks, "What?"
Ross is shown holding the phone to his ear, his mouth moving silently.
Monica says, "Okay."
Monica writes on a piece of paper resting on her lap.
Monica says, "All right."
Ross lowers the phone slightly, his gaze dropping to the floor.
In a living room, Rachel sits on a window seat with her knees drawn up, looking out the window at the rain. Monica walks toward a stereo system.
Monica turns a knob on the stereo. A male DJ's voice on the radio says, "You music."
Monica stands by the stereo with her arms crossed, looking toward Rachel. An instrumental rock song begins playing on the radio.
Rachel turns her head away from the window, resting her chin on her hand.
The DJ says, "The next one dedicated to Rachel from Ross." Rachel maintains her posture.

The DJ continues, "Rachel, he wants you to know he's deeply sorry for what he did and he hopes you can find it in your heart to forgive him. See the" Rachel turns her head back toward the window as the song's vocals begin.
The setting changes to a different living room where Ross sits on a couch, holding a green bottle. The song continues, "stone sick in your eyes see the thorn twist in your side"
Ross sets the bottle down on the table in front of him.
Ross slumps back against the couch cushions, crossing his arms. The song sings, "I wait"
Ross keeps his arms crossed, staring forward.
The song sings, "for you"
The setting returns to Rachel by the window. She reaches for a red telephone on a small cabinet beside her and picks up the receiver.
Rachel presses a button on the phone and brings it to her ear. The song continues, "sleight of hand and twist of faith on a bed of nail she makes me wait" Monica touches her hair and smiles slightly.
Rachel holds the phone to her ear.
The song sings, "and I wait"
The setting returns to Ross on the couch, staring forward.
The song sings "without you," and then abruptly stops. The DJ says, "we've just gotten a call from Rachel and she told us what Ross did. It's"
The DJ continues, "pretty appalling. And Ross, if you're listening, I don't want to play your song anymore." Ross shifts forward, his eyes widening.
Ross drops his head back onto the couch, closing his eyes.
The setting returns to Monica's apartment. Monica stands with her arms crossed, and Rachel remains on the window seat, looking out.

The DJ says, "Why don't we devote our time to a couple that stands a chance? Avery, Michelle's sorry she hit you with her car and she hopes you two," The setting returns to Ross, who stands up and walks away from the couch.
The DJ finishes, "will work it out there" as a new song begins playing. Ross walks toward a sliding glass door covered in raindrops.
Ross stands at the glass door, looking out at the rain.
The song sings, "where I can go" as Ross continues to stare out the window.
Ross remains motionless by the glass door.
The song sings, "hell secret to" The setting changes to show Rachel from the outside, looking through a rain-covered window pane.
Rachel leans her head against the window frame, looking out.
The song sings, "my room" Rachel blinks slowly.
Rachel continues to stare out the rain-streaked window.
The song sings, "in my" as more of the window frame and the surrounding room become visible.
The visible area of the room continues to widen.
The song completes the line with "room"
The wide view of Rachel's window remains as the rain continues to fall.
The view of the window lingers before the scene turns entirely black.
In an office, Mr. Rastatter sits at his desk. Monica enters, and he smiles, saying, "hi. Thanks for coming in again."
Monica approaches, shakes his hand, and replies, "Oh, not at all have no morals, and I need the cash like I'm looking in a mirror."
Mr. Rastatter picks up a small glass jar from his desk.
Holding the jar up, Mr. Rastatter says, "Anyway, they're called pistachios."
Monica looks at the jar in his hand.
Mr. Rastatter gestures with the jar and says, "Now, they taste exactly like pistachio goes but they're made primarily of reconstituted fish bits."
He unscrews the lid of the jar.
Mr. Rastatter holds the open jar out to Monica and says, "Here, try one."
Monica reaches into the jar.
Monica takes a piece from the jar and says, "All right."
Monica brings the piece toward her mouth.
Mr. Rastatter raises a finger and asks, "You're not allergic to anything, are you?" Monica pauses and replies, "Cat hair." Mr. Rastatter lowers his eyebrows and says, "Oh, sorry."
Monica freezes, looking down at the piece in her hand. The scene turns black.
The scene remains completely black.
